title: A Straw Hat Refuses to Fall
description: On pages 9–12, this part focuses on a straw hat refuses to fall, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: A Straw Hat Refuses to Fall. The Straw Hats’ victories against Baroque Works begin changing the momentum in Alubarna. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: A weapon!? Does some thing like that...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Really exist in this kingdom?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: A secret passed down from king to his sub- censor for genera- tions...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Once I become the king of this land,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: It'll only be too easy to bring all the pirates in nearby regions under my control!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: With such influence, I'll have the power to surpass even the World Government's authority itself! And at last, I will have my utopia!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Do you really think the World Gov- ermment will just let that happen!?

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Oh, of course not. That's precisely why I need some- thing to tip the scales in my favour... Pluton.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I don't know where you managed to learn that name,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But I'll have you know that I have no clue to its whereabouts,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Nor am I even sure if such a thing really exists in this kingdom.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: ...I see.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I expected as much.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Even I realize just how dubious the existence of such an incredible weapon is...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: In any case, the bulk of the royalist forces are currently gathered around the city square,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: We need to get inside!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: But at 4:30p.m... That is to say, in just 30 minutes from now, they'll be enquilfed by a massive explosion.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: We've gotta do something about those hands coming out from the doors!

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: Are you out of your mind!?

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: A special-made explosive with a blast diameter of 5km.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: The scenery viewable from here will undergo quite the "renovation," I'd assume...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: A skm blast in just 30 minutes!?

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: But if you do that...!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Happy, are you? The rebellion you so wanted to stop will finally be over.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: By my calculations, the rebel forces should penetrate all the way into the city square in about 20 minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Rather than blowing up the royal palace to stop the fighting in such a roundabout way,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's far more expedient to eliminate the ones doing the actual fighting.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: How can you do such a thing!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Princess!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Just what have my people ever done to you!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: ...What an ear sore.

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Now then, Mr. Cobra...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I have an alternative request for you.

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Where is the "Poneglyph?"

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The ancient records...!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Why would you want to know where that is...?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: If I tell you where it is, will you...

summary: This part develops a straw hat refuses to fall and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
